human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for electronics-and-appliance customer-support boundary discipline, source quality, order and ticket evidence handling, refund and service-recovery exception posture, safety and fraud escalation logic, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to electronics and appliance customer-support resolution, customer communication, and escalation packaging grounded in order, ticket, payment, warranty, and inventory records.

Authority sources cover FTC order and warranty guidance, California refund-law baseline, CFPB billing-dispute guidance, CPSC recall handling, PHMSA reverse-logistics safety constraints, and FTC Red Flags fraud-control guidance.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope resolution, missing facts, policy-exception asks, payment mismatch, recall and fraud escalation, high-volume queue handling, cross-lane handoff, repeat failure escalation, terminology fidelity, conflicting-policy handling, and tenant adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/consumer-electronics-and-appliance-retail-and-service/customer-support-electronics-specialist/ so support-resolution work stays separate from pricing, operations recovery, fraud adjudication, repair intake, warranty fulfillment, and safety ownership.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around refund exceptions, pricing overrides, repeat-failure complaints, fraud signals, and recalled-product safety questions.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated consumer-electronics-and-appliance customer-support specialist that provides evidence-backed resolution and disciplined escalation.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained-implementation runtime exists.
